item.title: Dental Crowns
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Crowns in Houston, TX

A dental crown is a tooth-shaped cap that covers a damaged, decayed, or cosmetically flawed tooth, restoring its strength, shape, and appearance. Dr. Philip J. Cimo offers porcelain, porcelain-fused-to-metal (PFM), and full-gold crowns depending on the location and function of the tooth. Porcelain crowns are the most popular choice for front teeth because they blend seamlessly with natural enamel.

The crown process typically takes two visits. During the first appointment, Dr. Cimo prepares the tooth by removing a thin layer of enamel and takes impressions. A temporary crown protects the tooth while your permanent restoration is crafted at a dental lab. At the second visit, the custom crown is cemented into place. With proper care, crowns can last 10–15 years or longer.

When Is a Crown Recommended?

  • Large cavities that cannot be filled
  • Cracked or fractured teeth
  • After root canal therapy
  • Worn or broken teeth
  • Cosmetic reshaping or discoloration

Call (713) 464-1887 or request an appointment online to find out if a crown is right for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dental Crowns in Houston
Restore damaged or decayed teeth with porcelain and PFM crowns from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Durable, natural-looking crowns that last 10–15 years.

item.title: Dental Bridges
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Bridges in Houston, TX

A dental bridge is a fixed prosthetic that replaces one or more missing teeth by anchoring an artificial tooth (pontic) to the natural teeth on either side of the gap. Bridges restore your ability to chew and speak normally, prevent remaining teeth from shifting, and maintain the shape of your face. Dr. Cimo offers traditional, cantilever, and Maryland bonded bridges.

The bridge procedure requires two visits. At the first appointment, the anchor teeth (abutments) are prepared and impressions are taken. A temporary bridge is placed while your permanent bridge is fabricated. During the second visit, the permanent bridge is carefully fitted and cemented. With good oral hygiene, bridges typically last 10–15 years.

Call (713) 464-1887 or schedule a consultation to discuss whether a bridge or dental implant is the best option for your missing tooth.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dental Bridges in Houston
Replace missing teeth with a natural-looking dental bridge from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Fixed, durable bridges that restore your smile and function.

item.title: Dentures
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dentures in Houston, TX

Dentures are removable appliances that replace missing teeth and surrounding gum tissue. Dr. Cimo provides both full (complete) dentures and partial dentures to restore a natural-looking smile and the ability to eat and speak comfortably. Modern dentures are crafted from high-quality acrylic and porcelain, closely mimicking the appearance of natural teeth and gums.

Types of Dentures We Offer

  • Conventional Full Dentures – Placed after all remaining teeth are removed and gum tissue has healed (8–12 weeks).
  • Immediate Full Dentures – Inserted the same day remaining teeth are extracted; require relining once healing is complete.
  • Partial Dentures – Clip onto remaining healthy teeth to fill gaps left by multiple missing teeth.
  • Implant-Supported Dentures – Snap onto dental implants for superior stability and comfort.

Remove and rinse dentures after meals, brush daily with a soft brush, and soak overnight in a denture solution. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dentures in Houston TX
Restore your smile with full, partial, or implant-supported dentures from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Comfortable, natural-looking removable appliances.

item.title: Porcelain Veneers
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Porcelain Veneers in Houston, TX

Porcelain veneers are ultra-thin ceramic shells custom-crafted to bond to the front surface of your teeth, instantly transforming stained, chipped, misaligned, or unevenly spaced teeth into a beautiful, uniform smile. Dr. Cimo designs each veneer to complement your facial features and natural tooth color.

The veneer process typically requires two to three visits. First, a small amount of enamel is removed from the tooth's surface, and impressions are taken. Temporary veneers protect your teeth while the lab fabricates your permanent restorations over 10–14 days. At the bonding appointment, Dr. Cimo checks the fit and color, then permanently adheres the veneers with a light-cured dental cement.

Veneers Can Correct

  • Severe staining or discoloration
  • Chipped or worn enamel
  • Gaps between teeth (diastema)
  • Slightly misaligned or uneven teeth
  • Short or irregularly shaped teeth

With proper care, porcelain veneers last 10–20 years. Contact us at (713) 464-1887 to schedule your smile consultation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Porcelain Veneers in Houston
Transform your smile with custom porcelain veneers from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Fix chips, stains, gaps, and uneven teeth with beautiful, lasting results.

item.title: Teeth Whitening
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Teeth Whitening in Houston, TX

A brighter smile can boost your confidence and make a lasting first impression. Dr. Cimo offers professional take-home whitening trays that deliver far superior results compared to over-the-counter strips. Custom-fitted trays ensure the whitening gel contacts every surface of your teeth evenly, with fewer side effects like sensitivity.

To begin, impressions are taken at our office and custom trays are fabricated. You'll receive professional-strength whitening gel and instructions for use. Most patients wear the trays for 30–60 minutes per day for 10–14 days, achieving results up to 4–8 shades whiter. For faster results, ask about our in-office ZOOM! whitening treatment.

Whitening Tips

  • Avoid coffee, tea, red wine, and tobacco during treatment
  • Use a sensitivity toothpaste if needed
  • Touch up every 6–12 months to maintain results

Call (713) 464-1887 to get started on your brighter smile today.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Teeth Whitening in Houston
Professional teeth whitening from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Custom take-home trays and in-office ZOOM! whitening for a smile up to 8 shades brighter.

item.title: ZOOM! Whitening
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
ZOOM! In-Office Whitening in Houston, TX

ZOOM! is the most popular in-office whitening system in the world — and for good reason. In just one appointment of about 60–90 minutes, Dr. Cimo can brighten your smile by 6–10 shades. A low-intensity light activates the hydrogen peroxide gel applied to your teeth, breaking down stains deep within the enamel and dentin.

The procedure begins with a thorough cleaning to remove surface plaque. Your lips and gums are protected with a covering, and the ZOOM! gel is applied in three 15-minute sessions under the activating light. Most patients experience little to no sensitivity, and you leave with a dramatically whiter smile the same day.

ZOOM! vs. Take-Home Whitening

  • ZOOM! – Results in a single 60-minute visit; ideal before weddings, events, or when time is short
  • Take-Home Trays – Gradual whitening over 10–14 days; convenient for maintenance

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your ZOOM! appointment and walk out with a brilliantly white smile.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | ZOOM Whitening in Houston TX
Get a smile up to 10 shades whiter in one visit with ZOOM! in-office whitening at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Fast, safe, and dramatic results.

item.title: Bonding & White Fillings
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Bonding & White Fillings in Houston, TX

Composite resin bonding is a versatile, affordable cosmetic and restorative treatment that can repair chips, close gaps, cover stains, and fill cavities — all in a single visit. Dr. Cimo uses a tooth-colored resin material that is sculpted directly onto the tooth and hardened with an LED curing light, creating a seamless result that blends with your natural enamel.

White (composite) fillings have largely replaced traditional metal amalgam fillings. They require less removal of healthy tooth structure, bond directly to the tooth for added strength, and are virtually invisible. The same-day procedure requires no lab work and no temporary restoration.

Bonding Can Correct

  • Chipped or cracked teeth
  • Small gaps between teeth
  • Discolored or stained teeth
  • Cavities (tooth-colored fillings)
  • Slightly misshapen teeth

With proper care, bonding lasts 5–10 years. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Bonding & White Fillings Houston
Repair chips, close gaps, and fill cavities with tooth-colored composite bonding from Dr. Cimo in Houston, TX. Natural-looking results in a single visit.

item.title: Inlays & Onlays
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Inlays & Onlays in Houston, TX

Inlays and onlays — sometimes called "indirect fillings" or "partial crowns" — are custom-made restorations used when decay or damage is too extensive for a direct filling but not severe enough to require a full crown. Dr. Cimo fabricates inlays and onlays from high-strength porcelain or composite resin for a durable, natural-looking result.

An inlay fits within the cusps of a tooth (similar to a filling), while an onlay extends over one or more cusps. Both are bonded to the tooth for a precise, tight fit that strengthens the remaining tooth structure. The procedure requires two visits: tooth preparation and impressioning at the first visit, and permanent bonding at the second.

Benefits Over Traditional Fillings

  • Greater strength and durability (10–30 years)
  • More conservative than a full crown
  • Perfect color match to natural teeth
  • Easy to clean — no gaps or overhangs

Call (713) 464-1887 to find out if an inlay or onlay is the right restoration for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Inlays & Onlays in Houston TX
Restore damaged back teeth with custom porcelain inlays and onlays from Dr. Cimo in Houston, TX. A durable, conservative alternative to full dental crowns.

item.title: Lumineers
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Lumineers in Houston, TX

Lumineers are an ultra-thin brand of porcelain veneer — about as thin as a contact lens — that can be placed with little to no removal of existing tooth structure. Because minimal enamel preparation is needed, the process is reversible in most cases, making Lumineers an appealing option for patients who want a dramatic smile transformation without permanently altering their teeth.

Using patented Cerinate porcelain, Lumineers are custom-crafted to match your desired tooth color and shape. They are cemented directly onto the front of your existing teeth, covering stains, chips, cracks, and gaps. Two appointments are typically all that is needed — one for impressions and one for placement.

Lumineers vs. Traditional Veneers

  • No shots or drilling required in most cases
  • Reversible (no permanent enamel removal)
  • Comparable durability — 20+ years with proper care
  • Similar aesthetic results for mild to moderate corrections

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation and see if Lumineers are right for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Lumineers in Houston TX
Transform your smile with no-prep Lumineers from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Ultra-thin veneers that require little to no enamel removal for a stunning smile.

item.title: Snap-On Smile
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Snap-On Smile in Houston, TX

Snap-On Smile is an innovative, non-invasive, removable arch that fits directly over your existing teeth to give you a beautiful, natural-looking smile — no shots, no drilling, no permanent change to your teeth. It is custom-crafted from a thin but strong dental resin and available in a variety of shades and styles to complement your face and personal aesthetic.

The process takes just two easy visits. At the first appointment, Dr. Cimo takes impressions and helps you select your preferred tooth shape and shade. About three weeks later, your Snap-On Smile is ready to try on and take home. Simply snap it in over your teeth whenever you want a picture-perfect smile.

Ideal for Patients Who

  • Want a temporary cosmetic improvement while saving for permanent treatment
  • Are not candidates for veneers or bonding
  • Have gaps, staining, chips, or missing teeth
  • Want a removable cosmetic solution

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn more about Snap-On Smile today.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Snap-On Smile Houston TX
Get a removable cosmetic smile transformation with Snap-On Smile from Dr. Cimo in Houston, TX. No drilling, no shots — a beautiful smile in just two visits.

item.title: BriteSmile Whitening
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
BriteSmile Teeth Whitening in Houston, TX

BriteSmile is a professional in-office teeth whitening system that uses a patented blue light technology combined with a gentle hydrogen peroxide gel to brighten your smile safely and effectively. The treatment takes approximately one hour and can lighten your teeth by several shades in a single visit.

Unlike over-the-counter whitening products, BriteSmile is performed under Dr. Cimo's supervision using professional-strength whitening agents. The process is gentle on enamel and gums, and most patients experience minimal sensitivity. Touch-up trays can be provided for at-home maintenance to extend your results.

If you are looking for a safe, fast, and effective way to brighten your smile before a special event or simply to boost your confidence, call our Houston office at (713) 464-1887 to schedule your BriteSmile appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | BriteSmile Whitening Houston
Professional BriteSmile in-office teeth whitening from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Brighter smile in about one hour with minimal sensitivity.

item.title: Clear Braces
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Clear Braces / Invisalign in Houston, TX

Clear aligner therapy offers a discreet way to straighten teeth without metal wires and brackets. Using a series of custom-made, virtually invisible plastic aligners, Dr. Cimo can correct mild to moderate orthodontic issues including crowding, spacing, and bite problems. Each aligner is worn for about two weeks before advancing to the next in the series.

Aligners are removable for eating, drinking, brushing, and flossing — a major advantage over traditional braces. Most treatment plans range from 6–18 months depending on the complexity of your case. Retainers are provided upon completion to maintain your new smile.

Benefits of Clear Aligners

  • Nearly invisible — most people won't notice them
  • Removable for meals and oral hygiene
  • No metal brackets or wires to irritate soft tissue
  • Fewer office visits than traditional braces

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a free consultation and 3D digital scan to see your projected results.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Clear Braces Invisalign Houston
Straighten your teeth discreetly with clear aligner therapy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Virtually invisible, removable, and comfortable orthodontics.

item.title: Traditional Braces
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Traditional Braces in Houston, TX

Traditional metal braces remain one of the most effective orthodontic treatments available, capable of correcting even complex tooth misalignment, crowding, spacing, and bite issues. Modern metal brackets are smaller, lighter, and more comfortable than ever, and many patients now choose braces with colored elastics for a personalized look.

Dr. Cimo works with orthodontic specialists in the Houston area to coordinate comprehensive treatment plans. Braces typically apply gentle continuous pressure via wires threaded through brackets bonded to each tooth, gradually guiding teeth into their ideal positions over 18–36 months. Regular adjustments every 4–8 weeks are part of the process.

Conditions Treated with Braces

  • Crowded or crooked teeth
  • Gaps and spacing
  • Overbite, underbite, crossbite, and open bite
  • Jaw alignment issues

Call (713) 464-1887 for a referral or consultation about orthodontic options for you or your child.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Traditional Braces Houston TX
Effective orthodontic treatment with traditional metal braces coordinated by Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Correct crowding, spacing, and bite issues.

item.title: Restorative Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Restorative Dentistry in Houston, TX

Restorative dentistry focuses on repairing and replacing damaged, decayed, or missing teeth to restore full oral function and a healthy smile. Dr. Philip J. Cimo offers a comprehensive range of restorative services using the latest materials and techniques — all designed to feel natural, look great, and last for years.

Our Restorative Services Include

  • Dental Crowns – Cap and protect damaged teeth
  • Dental Bridges – Replace one or more missing teeth
  • Dentures – Full and partial removable prosthetics
  • Dental Implants – Permanent tooth replacement
  • Root Canal Therapy – Save infected teeth
  • Inlays & Onlays – Partial crowns for back teeth
  • Tooth-Colored Fillings – Cavity repair without metal

Whether you need a single filling or a full-mouth rehabilitation, Dr. Cimo and his team will create a personalized treatment plan that fits your needs, timeline, and budget. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your restorative consultation today.

Comprehensive restorative dentistry from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Crowns, bridges, implants, dentures, fillings, and more to restore your smile.

item.title: Cosmetic Services
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cosmetic Dentistry in Houston, TX

A beautiful smile can transform the way you look and feel. Dr. Philip J. Cimo offers a full range of cosmetic dental services designed to improve the color, shape, size, and alignment of your teeth. Whether you want a subtle refresh or a complete smile makeover, our team will design a personalized plan to achieve your goals.

Our Cosmetic Services Include

  • Porcelain Veneers – Cover imperfections with thin ceramic shells
  • Lumineers – No-prep veneers for a reversible transformation
  • ZOOM! Whitening – Brighten your smile by up to 10 shades in one visit
  • Take-Home Whitening – Gradual whitening with custom trays
  • Dental Bonding – Repair chips and close gaps
  • Snap-On Smile – Removable cosmetic overlay
  • Clear Braces – Discreet orthodontic alignment
  • Smile Gallery – View before and after results

Schedule your complimentary smile consultation at (713) 464-1887 or request an appointment online.

item.title: Dental Implants
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Implants in Houston, TX

Dental implants are the gold standard for replacing missing teeth. A titanium post is surgically placed into the jawbone, where it fuses with bone tissue through a process called osseointegration over 3–6 months. Once the implant has integrated, a custom porcelain crown is attached — creating a permanent, natural-looking, and fully functional replacement that can last a lifetime with proper care.

Unlike bridges, implants do not require altering adjacent healthy teeth. Unlike dentures, implants are fixed in place and function just like natural teeth. They also stimulate the jawbone, preventing the bone loss that occurs when teeth are missing.

The Implant Process

  1. Consultation, imaging (i-CAT 3D scan), and treatment planning
  2. Implant placement surgery under local anesthesia or sedation
  3. Healing period (3–6 months for osseointegration)
  4. Abutment placement and final crown delivery

Call (713) 464-1887 or visit our Implant FAQs page to learn more.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dental Implants in Houston TX
Permanent tooth replacement with dental implants from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Natural-looking, lifetime results that preserve your jawbone.

item.title: Dental Implants
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Implants in Houston, TX

Dental implants are the most advanced and long-lasting solution for replacing missing teeth. A small titanium screw is placed into the jawbone and, over time, fuses with the surrounding bone. A natural-looking porcelain crown is then attached to create a permanent tooth replacement that looks, feels, and functions just like your own tooth.

Dr. Cimo has placed and restored hundreds of dental implants for patients across the Houston area. Using advanced 3D imaging (i-CAT) and the SimPlant planning software, he ensures precise, predictable placement for optimal outcomes. Whether you need to replace a single tooth or require a full-arch restoration, we have a solution for you.

Visit our Dental Implants page for complete information, or call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your implant consultation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Implants in Houston TX
Replace missing teeth permanently with dental implants from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Advanced 3D-guided implant placement for lasting, natural results.

item.title: Mini Dental Implants
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Mini Dental Implants in Houston, TX

Mini dental implants (MDIs) are a smaller-diameter version of conventional implants, ideal for patients who have insufficient bone density for standard implants or who need a more affordable, less invasive option. MDIs are most commonly used to stabilize lower dentures, preventing slipping and allowing patients to eat and speak with confidence.

The placement procedure for mini implants is minimally invasive — often completed in a single appointment with local anesthesia and no incisions or stitches. The small titanium posts are inserted directly through the gum tissue and into the bone. After a shorter healing period than conventional implants, your denture or prosthetic can be secured to the implants.

Benefits of Mini Implants

  • Less invasive than conventional implants
  • Can often be placed in a single visit
  • Ideal for stabilizing loose lower dentures
  • More affordable than full-size implants
  • Suitable for patients with bone loss

Call (713) 464-1887 to find out if mini implants are right for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Mini Dental Implants Houston
Stabilize loose dentures with mini dental implants from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. A less-invasive, affordable implant option with a quick recovery.

item.title: Root Canal Therapy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Root Canal Therapy in Houston, TX

Root canal therapy removes infected or inflamed pulp tissue from inside the tooth, relieves pain, and saves the natural tooth from extraction. Despite its reputation, a modern root canal is no more uncomfortable than getting a filling. Dr. Cimo uses rotary endodontic instruments and digital X-rays to ensure a thorough, comfortable procedure.

During treatment, the tooth is numbed with local anesthesia. Dr. Cimo creates a small opening in the crown of the tooth, removes the infected pulp, cleans and shapes the root canals, and fills them with a biocompatible material called gutta percha. A crown is typically placed afterward to protect and strengthen the treated tooth.

Signs You May Need a Root Canal

  • Severe toothache when chewing or applying pressure
  • Prolonged sensitivity to hot or cold
  • Darkening of the tooth
  • Swelling or tenderness in nearby gums
  • A persistent pimple on the gums

Call (713) 464-1887 for prompt care — early treatment can save your tooth and eliminate pain quickly.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Root Canal Therapy Houston TX
Save an infected tooth with comfortable root canal therapy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Relieve pain and preserve your natural tooth in one or two visits.

item.title: Endodontics
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Endodontics in Houston, TX

Endodontics is the dental specialty focused on the diagnosis and treatment of diseases and injuries affecting the dental pulp (the soft tissue inside the tooth containing nerves and blood vessels). Dr. Cimo provides a full range of endodontic services, helping patients keep their natural teeth for life rather than resorting to extraction.

Endodontic Services We Offer

  • Root Canal Therapy – Treatment of infected or inflamed pulp
  • Endodontic Retreatment – Re-treatment of a previously root-canaled tooth
  • Apicoectomy – Surgical removal of the root tip when retreatment is not sufficient
  • Rotary Endodontics – Nickel-titanium instruments for faster, more precise cleaning

Using an endodontic microscope and digital imaging, Dr. Cimo achieves exceptional precision and thoroughness in every endodontic procedure. If you are experiencing tooth pain, swelling, or sensitivity, call (713) 464-1887 for a prompt evaluation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Endodontics in Houston TX
Expert endodontic care from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Root canals, retreatment, apicoectomy, and rotary endodontics to save your natural teeth.

item.title: Endodontic Retreatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Endodontic Retreatment in Houston, TX

Occasionally, a tooth that has had root canal therapy fails to heal properly or becomes re-infected months or years after treatment. Endodontic retreatment gives that tooth a second chance. Dr. Cimo reopens the tooth, removes the existing filling material, carefully re-cleans and reshapes the canals, and refills and reseals them to promote healing.

Retreatment may be necessary if new decay develops, a crown was not placed promptly after the original treatment, or a new crack or fracture has exposed the root canals. In some cases, Dr. Cimo may recommend an apicoectomy (surgical retreatment) if non-surgical retreatment is not feasible.

Signs Retreatment May Be Needed

  • Return of pain or sensitivity in a previously treated tooth
  • New swelling, tenderness, or abscess near the tooth
  • Dark discoloration of the tooth
  • Persistent or new infection visible on X-ray

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule an evaluation and discuss your options.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Endodontic Retreatment Houston
Re-treat a failing root canal and save your tooth with endodontic retreatment from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Expert care for complex endodontic cases.

item.title: Apicoectomy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Apicoectomy (Endodontic Surgery) in Houston, TX

An apicoectomy is a minor surgical procedure performed when root canal therapy or retreatment has not resolved persistent infection at the root tip. Dr. Cimo makes a small incision in the gum tissue to access the end of the root, removes the infected tissue and the root tip itself, then seals the canal with a small filling. The gum is sutured closed and heals over a few weeks.

This procedure is typically completed under local anesthesia in about 30–90 minutes. Most patients experience only mild discomfort for a few days afterward and can return to normal activities the next day. Using an endodontic microscope, Dr. Cimo achieves the precision needed for successful outcomes even in complex cases.

When Is an Apicoectomy Needed?

  • Infection at the root tip that does not respond to root canal therapy
  • Canals that are calcified and cannot be retreated conventionally
  • Fractures near the root tip
  • Persistent symptoms after multiple root canal treatments

Call (713) 464-1887 for a consultation if you have a tooth that has not healed after root canal treatment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Apicoectomy in Houston TX
Resolve persistent root tip infection with an apicoectomy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Precise endodontic microsurgery to save your tooth.

item.title: Rotary Endodontics
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Rotary Endodontics in Houston, TX

Rotary endodontics uses electrically powered nickel-titanium (NiTi) instruments to clean and shape root canals more efficiently and precisely than hand files alone. The flexible NiTi instruments can navigate even curved canals without risk of breaking, and the motorized rotation allows Dr. Cimo to complete root canal treatment faster and with greater accuracy.

Benefits for patients include shorter appointment times, smoother-feeling root canal walls (which improves the seal of the filling material), and less discomfort during and after the procedure. Rotary endodontics is a standard part of Dr. Cimo's root canal treatment protocol, ensuring every patient receives the highest level of care.

Why Choose Rotary Endodontics?

  • Faster treatment — fewer appointments needed
  • Greater precision in curved or narrow canals
  • Smoother preparation for better sealing
  • Less stress on the tooth structure

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your root canal consultation with Dr. Cimo.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Rotary Endodontics Houston TX
Faster, more precise root canals with rotary endodontic instruments at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. NiTi files for smoother canals and better outcomes.

item.title: Periodontal / Gum Disease Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Periodontal & Gum Disease Treatment in Houston, TX

Periodontal (gum) disease is a bacterial infection of the gum tissue and bone that support your teeth. It progresses from gingivitis (mild inflammation) to periodontitis (bone and tissue destruction) if left untreated. Research links advanced gum disease to systemic conditions including heart disease, diabetes, stroke, and complications in pregnancy.

Dr. Cimo offers comprehensive periodontal treatment including scaling and root planing, Arestin antibiotic therapy, osseous surgery, and ongoing periodontal maintenance. Early-stage disease can often be managed non-surgically; advanced cases may require surgical intervention.

Warning Signs of Gum Disease

  • Red, swollen, or bleeding gums
  • Persistent bad breath
  • Receding gum line
  • Loose or shifting teeth
  • Pain when chewing

Don't wait — call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a periodontal evaluation. Early treatment is key to preventing permanent damage.

item.title: Periodontic Services
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Periodontic Services in Houston, TX

Periodontics encompasses all dental procedures focused on preventing, diagnosing, and treating diseases affecting the gums, bone, and supporting structures of the teeth. Dr. Cimo provides a comprehensive suite of periodontal services for patients ranging from those with mild gingivitis to those with advanced periodontitis requiring surgical intervention.

Our Periodontic Services

  • Gum Disease Treatment
  • Scaling & Root Planing
  • Osseous Surgery
  • Osseous Grafting
  • Gum Grafting
  • Soft Tissue Graft
  • Crown Lengthening
  • Cosmetic Periodontal Surgery
  • Periodontal Splinting
  • Arestin Antibiotic Therapy

Schedule your periodontal evaluation with Dr. Cimo by calling (713) 464-1887.

item.title: Scaling & Root Planing
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Scaling & Root Planing in Houston, TX

Scaling and root planing is the first-line non-surgical treatment for gum disease. "Scaling" removes plaque and calculus (tartar) from tooth surfaces and below the gum line, while "root planing" smooths the root surfaces to discourage bacterial reattachment and promote healing of the gum tissue. The procedure is typically done one quadrant at a time under local anesthesia.

After scaling and root planing, patients are placed on a periodontal maintenance schedule (typically every 3–4 months) to monitor healing and prevent disease recurrence. In many cases, this non-surgical approach is sufficient to control gum disease and avoid the need for surgery. Arestin antibiotic microspheres may be placed in deeper pockets immediately after the procedure to enhance results.

What to Expect After Treatment

  • Some gum soreness and sensitivity for 1–3 days
  • Slight bleeding with brushing for a few days
  • Gums may appear to recede slightly as swelling resolves
  • Improvement in bleeding, pocket depths, and attachment at follow-up

Call (713) 464-1887 if you have signs of gum disease. Early treatment delivers the best outcomes.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Scaling & Root Planing Houston

item.title: Osseous Surgery
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Osseous Surgery in Houston, TX

Osseous surgery (also called pocket reduction surgery) is a surgical periodontal procedure used when gum disease has caused significant bone loss around the teeth and pockets are too deep to be adequately cleaned with scaling and root planing alone. Dr. Cimo folds back the gum tissue, removes disease-causing bacteria, and reshapes the damaged bone to reduce pocket depth and create an environment that is easier to keep clean.

The procedure is performed under local anesthesia, and patients typically return to normal activities within a few days. After surgery, patients are placed on a periodontal maintenance program to monitor healing and prevent recurrence. Osseous surgery significantly reduces pocket depths, stabilizes the teeth, and stops further bone loss.

Benefits of Osseous Surgery

  • Eliminates deep bacteria-harboring pockets
  • Halts further bone and tissue destruction
  • Makes home care more effective
  • Can save teeth that might otherwise be extracted

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn if osseous surgery is indicated for your periodontal condition.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Osseous Surgery Houston TX
Treat advanced gum disease with osseous surgery from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Pocket reduction surgery to stop bone loss and save your teeth.

item.title: Osseous Grafting
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Osseous Grafting in Houston, TX

Osseous grafting (bone grafting for the jaw) rebuilds bone that has been lost due to periodontal disease, infection, or tooth extraction. Dr. Cimo places bone graft material — which may be the patient's own bone, donor bone, or a synthetic substitute — into the defect, where it acts as a scaffold for the body's own bone cells to regenerate and fill in the area over several months.

Osseous grafting is often used in conjunction with osseous surgery to fill bone craters created by gum disease, or following tooth extraction to preserve the socket for a future implant. The procedure is performed under local anesthesia with sedation available, and most patients experience only mild soreness for a few days.

Common Indications

  • Bone craters from advanced periodontitis
  • Socket preservation after tooth extraction
  • Ridge augmentation before implant placement
  • Infrabony periodontal defects

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn if osseous grafting can restore your jawbone and support long-term dental health.

item.title: Gum Grafting
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Gum Grafting in Houston, TX

Gum recession exposes the sensitive root surfaces of teeth, leading to sensitivity, an uneven smile, and increased risk of decay and bone loss. Gum grafting restores receded gum tissue using a graft taken from the roof of your mouth (palate) or a donor source, protecting the root and improving both function and aesthetics.

Dr. Cimo uses microsurgical techniques to minimize patient discomfort and optimize healing. The graft is sutured into place over the exposed root, and within a few weeks, it integrates with the existing gum tissue. Most patients report only mild soreness at the donor and recipient sites for 1–2 weeks.

Reasons for Gum Recession

  • Aggressive tooth brushing
  • Periodontal disease
  • Thin gum tissue genetics
  • Orthodontic treatment
  • Teeth grinding (bruxism)

Call (713) 464-1887 if you notice your teeth looking longer or feel root sensitivity — gum grafting may be the solution.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Gum Grafting Houston TX
Restore receded gum tissue with gum grafting from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Protect exposed roots and improve your smile's appearance with microsurgical precision.

item.title: Soft Tissue Graft
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Soft Tissue Graft in Houston, TX

A soft tissue graft is a periodontal surgical procedure that adds volume to thin or receded gum tissue. Using connective tissue or epithelial tissue taken from the palate (or processed donor material), Dr. Cimo grafts the material to the affected area to cover exposed roots, thicken the gum tissue, and prevent further recession.

There are several types of soft tissue grafts: connective tissue grafts (most common), free gingival grafts (to increase the band of attached gum tissue), and pedicle grafts (using tissue adjacent to the recession site). Dr. Cimo will determine the most appropriate type based on your anatomy and clinical needs.

Benefits of Soft Tissue Grafts

  • Covers and protects exposed root surfaces
  • Reduces tooth sensitivity from recession
  • Improves gum line aesthetics
  • Prevents further recession and bone loss
  • Creates a more stable environment around teeth and implants

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your evaluation and find out which graft option is best for you.

item.title: Crown Lengthening
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Crown Lengthening in Houston, TX

Crown lengthening is a periodontal procedure that exposes more of the natural tooth structure by reshaping excess gum and bone tissue. It is performed either to facilitate restorative treatment (when a tooth is broken below the gum line and a crown is needed) or for cosmetic purposes to correct a "gummy smile" and create a more balanced, attractive gum line.

Under local anesthesia, Dr. Cimo carefully repositions the gum tissue and, when necessary, reshapes the supporting bone. The gums are sutured into their new position and heal over 4–6 weeks before restorative work is completed. For cosmetic crown lengthening, the results are immediate and can dramatically improve smile symmetry and proportions.

Crown Lengthening Is Used For

  • Teeth that are broken off at the gum line
  • Short clinical crowns that make restoration difficult
  • Gummy smile correction
  • Uneven gum levels affecting smile symmetry

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation for crown lengthening.

item.title: Cosmetic Periodontal Surgery
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Cosmetic Periodontal Surgery in Houston, TX

Cosmetic periodontal surgery reshapes the gum and bone tissue to enhance the aesthetics of your smile. Procedures including crown lengthening, gum contouring, gum grafting, and ridge augmentation can correct a gummy smile, uneven gum levels, black triangles between teeth, and other gum tissue irregularities that affect your smile's appearance.

Dr. Cimo designs each cosmetic periodontal treatment plan with both function and aesthetics in mind. Whether you want to correct a gummy smile before your wedding, cover receded gums that are making you self-conscious, or create a more uniform gum line for veneers, we can help achieve your smile goals.

Cosmetic Periodontal Procedures Include

  • Gum contouring and reshaping
  • Crown lengthening
  • Gum grafting for recession
  • Ridge augmentation after tooth loss
  • Frenectomy to correct lip or tongue ties

Schedule your smile consultation at (713) 464-1887 to explore how periodontal surgery can enhance your smile.

item.title: Periodontal Splinting
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Periodontal Splinting in Houston, TX

Periodontal splinting is a procedure that connects loose teeth together, distributing the forces of chewing across multiple teeth rather than a single compromised tooth. This stabilizes mobile teeth, reduces discomfort, and can help preserve teeth that would otherwise need to be extracted due to bone loss from advanced gum disease.

Dr. Cimo typically uses a fiber-reinforced composite resin to bond the inside surfaces of the teeth together, or in some cases, fabricates a removable splint. The procedure is conservative, comfortable, and can be combined with other periodontal treatments such as scaling and root planing or osseous surgery.

When Is Splinting Recommended?

  • Teeth loosened by advanced periodontal bone loss
  • Teeth loosened after trauma
  • As a temporary measure while waiting for gum treatment to take effect
  • To protect teeth from bruxism-related forces

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a periodontal consultation and find out if splinting can help save your teeth.

item.title: Arestin Antibiotic Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Arestin Antibiotic Treatment in Houston, TX

Arestin (minocycline hydrochloride) is a locally administered antibiotic used as an adjunct to scaling and root planing to treat gum disease. Immediately after the deep cleaning procedure, Dr. Cimo places tiny Arestin microspheres directly into infected periodontal pockets. The microspheres biodegrade slowly over several weeks, releasing the antibiotic directly at the site of infection at concentrations far higher than systemic oral antibiotics can achieve.

Clinical studies show that scaling and root planing combined with Arestin significantly reduces pocket depth and improves attachment levels compared to scaling and root planing alone. Arestin targets the bacteria responsible for gum disease while minimizing the side effects associated with systemic antibiotic therapy.

What to Expect After Arestin Placement

  • Avoid touching the treated areas with fingers, toothpicks, or other objects
  • Do not floss or use interdental cleaners for 10 days after placement
  • Avoid hard, crunchy foods for 1 week
  • Follow-up at 4–6 weeks to evaluate healing

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn how Arestin combined with deep cleaning can help control your gum disease.

item.title: Frenectomy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Frenectomy in Houston, TX

A frenectomy is the surgical removal or modification of the frenum — the small fold of tissue connecting the lip, cheek, or tongue to the gum tissue or floor of the mouth. When a frenum is too tight or too prominent, it can restrict tongue movement (tongue tie), cause a gap between the front teeth, or pull on the gum tissue contributing to recession.

Dr. Cimo performs frenectomies using a scalpel or soft-tissue laser for minimal bleeding and faster healing. The procedure takes only a few minutes under local anesthesia, and most patients heal completely within 1–2 weeks. Frenectomies are commonly performed in children to address tongue ties that interfere with speech or breastfeeding, and in adults to close a gap between the upper front teeth before orthodontic treatment.

Types of Frenectomy

  • Labial frenectomy – Removes the tissue between the upper lip and gums
  • Lingual frenectomy – Releases tongue tie for improved speech and function

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a frenectomy consultation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php

Correct tongue tie and lip tie with a frenectomy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Quick, comfortable procedure with laser or scalpel and fast healing.

item.title: Tooth Extractions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tooth Extractions in Houston, TX

While Dr. Cimo always prefers to save natural teeth whenever possible, there are situations where extraction is the best option — severely decayed or fractured teeth, advanced gum disease, teeth causing crowding before orthodontic treatment, or teeth that pose an infection risk. Dr. Cimo performs both simple extractions (fully erupted teeth) and surgical extractions (impacted or broken teeth) with care and precision.

Under local anesthesia (with sedation available for anxious patients), the tooth is loosened from the ligament and gently removed. Post-operative instructions including gauze pressure, ice packs, and diet restrictions are provided to promote healing and prevent dry socket. Most patients recover within 1–3 days for simple extractions.

After Your Extraction

  • Bite on gauze for 30–45 minutes to control bleeding
  • Apply ice 20 minutes on/20 minutes off for the first day
  • Avoid straws, smoking, and hard foods for 3–5 days
  • Take prescribed pain medication as directed

Ask about dental implants or bridges to replace extracted teeth. Call (713) 464-1887.

Comfortable tooth extractions from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Simple and surgical extractions with sedation options for a stress-free experience.

item.title: Multiple Tooth Extractions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Multiple Tooth Extractions in Houston, TX

When multiple teeth need to be extracted due to advanced decay, gum disease, trauma, or preparation for dentures, Dr. Cimo performs the procedure in a single appointment whenever possible, minimizing the number of surgical visits required. IV sedation or oral conscious sedation is strongly recommended for multiple extraction appointments to maximize comfort.

Careful treatment planning ensures that extractions are sequenced to minimize trauma, preserve bone for future implants or dentures, and allow for placement of immediate temporaries or dentures the same day when needed. Post-operative care instructions are reviewed thoroughly, and follow-up appointments are scheduled to monitor healing.

Preparation for Multiple Extractions

  • Arrange for a driver if sedation is planned
  • Stock up on soft foods: yogurt, smoothies, mashed potatoes, soup
  • Fill any prescriptions before the appointment
  • Rest for 24–48 hours after the procedure

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation for multiple extractions and discuss your tooth replacement options.

item.title: Wisdom Teeth Removal
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Wisdom Teeth Removal in Houston, TX

Wisdom teeth (third molars) typically erupt in the late teens or early twenties. When there isn't enough room in the jaw for them to emerge properly, they can become impacted — trapped beneath the gum or bone — leading to pain, infection, crowding, and damage to adjacent teeth. Dr. Cimo recommends extraction when wisdom teeth pose a current or future risk.

The extraction procedure is performed under local anesthesia, with oral sedation or IV sedation available for patient comfort. Impacted teeth may require a small incision in the gum tissue and removal of surrounding bone. Dissolving sutures close the site. Most patients recover within 3–5 days with proper home care. Summer break is an ideal time for teens and young adults to have wisdom teeth removed.

Signs Your Wisdom Teeth May Need Removal

  • Pain or tenderness at the back of the mouth
  • Swollen, red, or bleeding gums near the wisdom teeth
  • Recurring infections in the gum tissue over a wisdom tooth (pericoronitis)
  • Crowding or shifting of other teeth
  • Cyst formation around an impacted tooth

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a wisdom tooth evaluation with panoramic X-ray.

Comfortable wisdom teeth removal from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Sedation options for impacted third molar extraction with quick 3–5 day recovery.

item.title: Bone Grafting
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Bone Grafting in Houston, TX

Bone grafting rebuilds the jawbone to create a solid foundation for dental implants or to address bone loss caused by gum disease, infection, or tooth loss. Dr. Cimo uses a variety of graft materials — the patient's own bone (autograft), donor bone (allograft), bovine-derived bone (xenograft), or synthetic materials (alloplast) — depending on the size of the defect and the patient's needs.

The graft material is placed in the deficient area and covered with a membrane to protect the site during healing. New bone formation typically occurs over 3–9 months. Once the graft has matured, dental implants can be placed with confidence in a dense, healthy bone foundation.

Types of Bone Grafts We Perform

  • Extraction site preservation (socket graft)
  • Ridge augmentation
  • Sinus augmentation/lift
  • Periodontal bone grafting

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a bone grafting consultation and determine if you are a candidate for dental implants.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Bone Grafting in Houston TX
Rebuild jawbone for dental implants with bone grafting from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Socket preservation, ridge augmentation, and sinus lifts.

item.title: Sinus Augmentation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sinus Augmentation (Sinus Lift) in Houston, TX

A sinus augmentation, or sinus lift, adds bone to the upper jaw in the area of the molars and premolars — between the jaw and the maxillary sinuses on either side of the nose. When upper back teeth are lost, the sinus cavity often expands downward due to bone resorption, leaving insufficient bone height for implant placement. A sinus lift creates the space needed for successful implants in the upper posterior region.

Dr. Cimo lifts the sinus membrane, places bone graft material in the newly created space, and allows 4–9 months of healing before implant placement. The procedure is performed under local anesthesia with sedation available. Most patients experience only mild discomfort, swelling, and nasal stuffiness for a week following surgery.

Signs You May Need a Sinus Lift

  • Missing upper back teeth for an extended period
  • Insufficient bone height shown on 3D imaging
  • Sinus proximity to the upper jaw identified during implant planning

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation and 3D CT scan to evaluate your bone volume.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Sinus Augmentation Houston TX
Create enough bone for upper implants with sinus augmentation from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Sinus lift surgery for patients with bone loss in the upper jaw.

item.title: Ridge Augmentation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Ridge Augmentation in Houston, TX

When a tooth is extracted and the socket is not preserved, or when bone has been lost over time due to disease or trauma, the alveolar ridge (the bony ridge of the jaw) can collapse and narrow. Ridge augmentation rebuilds this bony ridge to create a proper foundation for dental implants, bridges, or dentures, and to restore the natural contour of the jaw for aesthetic purposes.

Dr. Cimo places bone graft material into the deficient ridge area and covers it with a resorbable membrane. Over 4–9 months, the body replaces the graft with new bone, filling in the ridge to the necessary height and width. Ridge augmentation is often planned in advance of implant placement and may be combined with other grafting procedures.

Why Ridge Augmentation Matters

  • Creates adequate bone volume for implant placement
  • Restores natural jaw contours under a bridge or denture
  • Improves the appearance of the gum line following tooth loss
  • Prevents further bone resorption in the edentulous area

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn if ridge augmentation is part of your implant treatment plan.

Rebuild a collapsed jawbone ridge with ridge augmentation from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Restore bone volume for implants or to improve denture fit.

item.title: Extraction Site Preservation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Extraction Site Preservation in Houston, TX

After a tooth is extracted, the surrounding bone begins to resorb (shrink) rapidly — losing up to 40–60% of its volume within 2–3 years. Extraction site preservation (also called socket preservation or socket grafting) places bone graft material immediately into the empty socket at the time of extraction, maintaining bone volume and shape for a future implant or bridge.

Dr. Cimo strongly recommends socket preservation for any extraction site where a dental implant is planned. The graft material is covered with a collagen membrane and sutures. Healing takes 3–4 months, after which the implant can typically be placed into solid, well-formed bone without the need for additional grafting procedures.

Benefits of Socket Preservation

  • Maintains bone width and height for implant placement
  • Reduces need for additional bone grafting later
  • Shorter overall treatment timeline
  • Better implant integration and long-term stability

Ask Dr. Cimo about socket preservation at the time of your extraction consultation. Call (713) 464-1887.

item.title: Oral Biopsy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral Biopsy in Houston, TX

An oral biopsy is the removal of a small sample of tissue from the mouth for laboratory examination to determine whether an abnormal lesion is benign or malignant. Dr. Cimo recommends a biopsy whenever he observes an unusual sore, lump, white patch (leukoplakia), red patch (erythroplakia), or growth that does not resolve within two weeks.

The procedure is quick and performed under local anesthesia. A small piece of tissue is removed using a scalpel or punch biopsy instrument and sent to an oral pathology laboratory. Results are typically available within 7–10 business days. Early detection of oral cancer dramatically improves treatment outcomes and survival rates.

Lesions That May Require Biopsy

  • White or red patches that persist for more than 2 weeks
  • Unexplained lumps or growths in the mouth
  • Non-healing ulcers or sores
  • Suspicious findings identified during oral cancer screening

Don't delay — if you notice anything unusual in your mouth, call (713) 464-1887 for prompt evaluation.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Oral Biopsy in Houston TX
Identify suspicious oral lesions early with an oral biopsy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Prompt tissue sampling for accurate diagnosis and peace of mind.

item.title: Oral Pathology
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral Pathology in Houston, TX

The inside of a healthy mouth is lined with smooth, coral-pink mucosa. Any change in the appearance of this lining may signal a pathological process. Dr. Cimo screens for oral pathology at every recall visit, and encourages patients to perform a monthly self-examination of their lips, tongue, cheeks, gums, and floor of the mouth.

Warning Signs to Watch For

  • White or red patches inside the mouth
  • A sore that bleeds easily and does not heal within 2 weeks
  • A lump, thickening, or rough patch in soft tissue
  • Chronic sore throat or hoarseness
  • Difficulty chewing, swallowing, or moving the jaw or tongue

These changes can be detected during your regular dental exams. If Dr. Cimo identifies a suspicious lesion, he will recommend an oral cancer screening using advanced UV light technology, and if necessary, an oral biopsy for definitive diagnosis.

Your health and peace of mind are our priority. Call (713) 464-1887 if you notice any unusual changes in your oral tissues.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Oral Pathology Houston TX
Detect and diagnose oral disease early with oral pathology screening from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Identify suspicious lesions before they progress.

item.title: Canine Exposure
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Canine Exposure in Houston, TX

The upper canine teeth (cuspids) are the most important teeth in your dental arch for proper bite alignment and chewing function. When a canine tooth becomes impacted — unable to erupt through the gum on its own — surgical exposure is often recommended to guide it into its correct position in coordination with orthodontic treatment.

Dr. Cimo surgically uncovers the impacted canine by removing the gum tissue and sometimes a small amount of bone overlying the tooth. He then bonds a small orthodontic bracket to the exposed tooth, and an orthodontic wire guides the tooth into its proper position over the following months. This approach saves the natural canine, which is far preferable to extraction and replacement.

Why Save the Canine?

  • Canines provide critical guidance for your bite
  • Natural teeth are always preferable to prosthetics
  • Implants in the canine position are more complex and costly
  • Successful eruption leads to a complete, functional smile

Call (713) 464-1887 to coordinate with your orthodontist on canine exposure treatment.

Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Canine Exposure Houston TX
Guide impacted canine teeth into position with surgical exposure from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Save your natural canine and achieve a complete, aligned smile.

item.title: Facial Trauma
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Facial Trauma Treatment in Houston, TX

Injuries to the face — including fractured teeth, knocked-out (avulsed) teeth, jaw fractures, and lacerations to oral tissues — require prompt, expert attention. Dr. Cimo is trained in the management of dental and facial trauma and provides urgent care for traumatic injuries to the teeth, gums, and supporting bone structures.

Common Dental Trauma Situations

  • Avulsed (knocked-out) tooth – If a permanent tooth is knocked out, keep it moist (hold in cheek, milk, or saline) and get to our office within 30–60 minutes. Reimplantation is possible if treatment is prompt.
  • Fractured tooth – A broken tooth may need bonding, a crown, or extraction depending on the extent of the fracture.
  • Luxated tooth – A tooth that has been displaced or pushed into the socket can often be repositioned and stabilized with a splint.
  • Soft tissue lacerations – Cuts to the lips, cheeks, tongue, or gums may require suturing.

Dental emergencies require urgent care. Call (713) 464-1887 immediately if you or a family member experiences facial or dental trauma.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php

Urgent dental trauma care from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Knocked-out teeth, fractures, lacerations — call (713) 464-1887 for immediate treatment.

item.title: Orthognathic Surgery
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Orthognathic Surgery in Houston, TX

Orthognathic (jaw) surgery corrects skeletal irregularities of the jaw that affect the ability to eat, speak, and breathe properly, and that cannot be treated with orthodontics alone. Dr. Cimo works in close coordination with orthodontists and oral surgeons to plan and manage orthognathic treatment for patients with significant jaw misalignment.

Conditions Treated with Orthognathic Surgery

  • Severe overbite or underbite
  • Open bite — upper and lower teeth don't meet
  • Facial asymmetry from jaw discrepancies
  • Obstructive sleep apnea from jaw structure
  • Difficulty chewing or biting certain foods
  • Chronic jaw or TMJ pain related to skeletal discrepancy

Treatment typically involves 12–18 months of pre-surgical orthodontics to align the teeth within each jaw, followed by surgical repositioning of one or both jaws, and 6–12 months of post-surgical orthodontics to finalize the bite. Recovery from surgery typically takes 3–6 weeks.

Call (713) 464-1887 for a comprehensive evaluation and to discuss whether orthognathic surgery is appropriate for your condition.

item.title: Platelet Rich Plasma (PRP)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Platelet Rich Plasma (PRP) in Houston, TX

Platelet Rich Plasma (PRP) therapy uses a concentrated preparation of the patient's own blood platelets to accelerate healing and enhance tissue regeneration at surgical sites. Platelets contain growth factors that stimulate cell growth, new blood vessel formation, and bone and tissue repair. In dentistry, PRP is used in conjunction with bone grafts, implant surgery, and periodontal procedures.

A small amount of blood is drawn from the patient, processed in a centrifuge to concentrate the platelets, and applied to the surgical site at the time of the procedure. This natural, autologous treatment reduces post-operative discomfort, decreases healing time, and improves the predictability of graft outcomes.

PRP Is Used In

  • Bone grafting procedures
  • Dental implant placement
  • Osseous surgery
  • Sinus augmentation
  • Extraction site preservation

Ask Dr. Cimo whether PRP therapy is appropriate for your planned surgical procedure. Call (713) 464-1887.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | PRP Therapy Houston TX
Accelerate surgical healing with Platelet Rich Plasma (PRP) therapy from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Natural growth factors to enhance bone and tissue regeneration.

item.title: Sedation Dentistry
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation Dentistry in Houston, TX

Dental anxiety affects up to 40 million Americans and is one of the most common reasons people avoid necessary dental care. Dr. Cimo offers multiple levels of sedation so that every patient — regardless of their anxiety level — can receive the dental care they need in complete comfort. From mild nitrous oxide to deep IV sedation, we have a solution for you.

Sedation Options We Offer

  • Nitrous Oxide – Mild inhalation sedation; you remain fully conscious and alert
  • Oral Conscious Sedation – A prescribed pill taken before your appointment for moderate relaxation
  • IV Sedation – Deep sedation administered intravenously; ideal for complex procedures or severe anxiety

Sedation is also recommended for long appointments, patients with special needs, strong gag reflex, or those requiring extensive treatment in a single visit. All sedated patients must arrange for a driver to take them home.

Call (713) 464-1887 to discuss which sedation option is best for your needs. See our Sedation FAQs for common questions.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Sedation Dentistry Houston TX
Overcome dental anxiety with sedation dentistry from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Nitrous oxide, oral conscious sedation, and IV sedation for total comfort.

item.title: IV Sedation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
IV Sedation in Houston, TX

IV (intravenous) sedation delivers sedative medications directly into the bloodstream, producing a deep state of relaxation. Patients are typically in a twilight state — not fully asleep like general anesthesia, but deeply relaxed and with little to no memory of the procedure afterward. IV sedation is ideal for patients with severe dental anxiety, complex surgical procedures, or those who need extensive treatment completed in a single visit.

Dr. Cimo is trained and certified in IV sedation and monitors all vital signs throughout the procedure. A trained assistant assists throughout the appointment. The effects of IV sedation wear off within a few hours, but patients must arrange for a responsible adult to drive them home and stay with them for the remainder of the day.

Ideal for

  • Severe dental phobia or anxiety
  • Multiple extractions, implants, or bone grafts
  • Patients with strong gag reflex
  • Patients with special needs
  • Long or complex surgical procedures

Call (713) 464-1887 to discuss whether IV sedation is right for your appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | IV Sedation Dentistry Houston TX
Experience deep relaxation with IV sedation dentistry from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Wake up with your dental work done and little memory of the procedure.

item.title: Oral Conscious Sedation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral Conscious Sedation in Houston, TX

Oral conscious sedation uses a prescription anti-anxiety medication taken by mouth (usually a benzodiazepine such as Halcion or Valium) about an hour before your appointment. The medication produces a comfortable, drowsy, and deeply relaxed state. You remain conscious and can respond to Dr. Cimo's questions, but feel calm and at ease throughout the procedure. Many patients have little to no memory of the appointment.

Oral sedation is a step up in intensity from nitrous oxide and is appropriate for patients with moderate to severe dental anxiety, those requiring longer procedures, or patients who have not found nitrous oxide sufficient. A driver is required, as you will not be able to drive for the remainder of the day.

What to Expect

  • Take the prescribed pill 1 hour before your appointment
  • Arrange for a responsible adult driver
  • You may feel very drowsy and have amnesia for the appointment
  • Rest at home for the remainder of the day
  • Do not operate machinery or make important decisions until the next day

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn if oral conscious sedation is right for you.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Oral Sedation Dentistry Houston
Relax deeply with oral conscious sedation from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. A prescription pill taken before your appointment for a calm, anxiety-free visit.

item.title: Nitrous Oxide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Nitrous Oxide Sedation in Houston, TX

Nitrous oxide — commonly called "laughing gas" — is the mildest form of dental sedation. It is inhaled through a small mask that fits comfortably over the nose, producing a light, pleasant sense of relaxation and well-being within minutes. You remain fully conscious and able to communicate throughout the appointment. Nitrous oxide is safe for children and adults alike and wears off completely within minutes after the mask is removed.

Unlike oral or IV sedation, you can drive yourself home after a nitrous oxide appointment. It is ideal for patients with mild dental anxiety, those undergoing routine procedures, or children who need a little extra help staying calm. Nitrous oxide can also be combined with a local anesthetic injection for maximum comfort.

Benefits of Nitrous Oxide

  • Takes effect in 2–3 minutes and wears off quickly
  • Reduces anxiety and discomfort during procedures
  • Safe for children and adults
  • No recovery time — you can drive home
  • Adjustable level of sedation throughout the appointment

Ask about nitrous oxide at your next appointment. Call (713) 464-1887.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Nitrous Oxide Laughing Gas Houston
Relax during dental treatment with nitrous oxide (laughing gas) from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Mild, safe sedation for children and adults — no recovery needed.

item.title: Anxiolysis
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Anxiolysis Sedation in Houston, TX

Anxiolysis refers to the minimal level of sedation — just enough to relieve anxiety while keeping the patient fully awake, cooperative, and able to follow instructions. At this level, cognitive and physical coordination are essentially unimpaired. Nitrous oxide is the most common method used to achieve anxiolysis, but a low-dose oral medication may also be used.

Anxiolysis is appropriate for patients who experience mild nervousness about dental procedures but do not require deeper sedation. It takes the edge off without significantly impairing function or requiring a driver. Anxiolysis is a great first step for patients who are working up to more involved treatment.

Who Benefits from Anxiolysis?

  • Patients with mild dental nervousness
  • First-time dental patients who are unsure what to expect
  • Children who are apprehensive about treatment
  • Patients who need a simple relaxant for routine procedures

If anxiety has been keeping you from seeking dental care, call (713) 464-1887. We will find the right level of sedation to make your experience comfortable.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Anxiolysis Dental Sedation Houston
Relieve mild dental anxiety with anxiolysis sedation from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Minimal sedation to take the edge off without impairing function.

item.title: Sedation FAQs
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation Dentistry FAQs in Houston, TX

Have questions about sedation dentistry? Dr. Cimo and his team are happy to address any concerns you have about sedation before your appointment. Below are answers to the most common questions patients ask.

Is sedation safe?

Yes. All sedation is administered and monitored by Dr. Cimo, who is trained and certified in sedation techniques. Vital signs are monitored throughout the procedure, and reversal agents are available if needed.

Will I be unconscious?

It depends on the type of sedation. With nitrous oxide and oral conscious sedation, you remain conscious but deeply relaxed. With IV sedation, you enter a twilight state with little memory of the procedure — but you can still respond to verbal commands.

Will I feel pain?

No. Sedation is used in combination with local anesthetic. You will not feel pain during the procedure.

Do I need a driver?

Oral sedation and IV sedation require a responsible adult driver. Nitrous oxide does not require a driver as it wears off within minutes.

How much does sedation cost?

Sedation costs vary by type and duration. We will discuss fees at your consultation. Financing is available through CareCredit and Sunbit. Call (713) 464-1887 for details.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Sedation Dentistry FAQs Houston
Get answers to common questions about sedation dentistry from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Safe, comfortable sedation for anxious patients.

item.title: Sedation Q&A
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Sedation Dentistry Q&A in Houston, TX

Below are more in-depth questions and answers about sedation options at Brittmoore Dental. If you have a question not answered here, please call us at (713) 464-1887.

Who is a good candidate for sedation?

Anyone who experiences anxiety about dental visits, has a strong gag reflex, needs extensive work completed, has sensitive teeth, has difficulty getting numb with local anesthesia alone, or has special needs that make dental treatment challenging is a good candidate for sedation.

Can sedation be used for routine cleanings?

Yes. If dental anxiety prevents you from attending regular cleanings, sedation can be used even for routine hygiene appointments. It is far better to receive care with sedation than to avoid it altogether.

What medications are used for oral sedation?

Dr. Cimo typically prescribes Triazolam (Halcion) or Diazepam (Valium) for oral conscious sedation. These are benzodiazepine medications that produce relaxation and amnesia. They are taken about one hour before the appointment.

How long will I feel the effects of oral sedation?

The full effects typically last 4–6 hours. You will likely feel drowsy for most of the day. Plan to rest at home and avoid any important activities or decisions until the next morning.

Visit our Sedation Dentistry overview page or call (713) 464-1887 to discuss your sedation options.
