item.title: Before Anesthesia Instructions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Before Anesthesia Instructions

If you are scheduled for a procedure with IV sedation or oral conscious sedation at Brittmoore Dental, please follow these instructions carefully to ensure your safety and the success of your appointment.

Eating & Drinking

  • Nothing to eat or drink for 8 hours before your appointment (includes water, gum, and hard candy)
  • If you must take essential morning medications, take them with a small sip of water no later than 2 hours before your appointment

Transportation

  • You must arrange for a responsible adult to drive you to and from the office
  • You cannot drive for the remainder of the day following sedation
  • Ride-share services (Uber/Lyft) are acceptable only if accompanied by a responsible adult

Clothing & Jewelry

  • Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing with short sleeves for IV access
  • Remove contact lenses and leave jewelry at home
  • Avoid nail polish on fingers or toes if pulse oximetry will be used

Medications

  • Inform our office of all medications, vitamins, and supplements you take
  • Blood thinners may need to be paused — discuss with Dr. Cimo at your consultation

Questions? Call (713) 464-1887 before your appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | Before Anesthesia Instructions Houston
Pre-anesthesia instructions for patients scheduled for IV or oral sedation at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Follow these guidelines for a safe procedure.

item.title: Dental Hygiene
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Hygiene in Houston, TX

Professional dental hygiene care is the foundation of a healthy smile. At Brittmoore Dental, our hygienists provide thorough cleanings, gum evaluations, oral cancer screenings, and personalized home care instruction at every visit. Regular professional cleanings (every 6 months for healthy patients, every 3–4 months for periodontal patients) remove tartar buildup that cannot be removed by brushing and flossing alone.

What's Included in Your Hygiene Visit

  • Medical and dental history review and update
  • Blood pressure screening
  • Full-mouth periodontal charting (pocket depths, recession, bleeding)
  • Oral cancer screening
  • Digital X-rays as indicated
  • Removal of plaque and calculus above and below the gum line
  • Polishing to remove surface stains
  • Fluoride treatment (optional)
  • Personalized oral hygiene instruction

Don't wait until there's a problem. Consistent preventive care is the most effective and economical approach to long-term dental health. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your cleaning today.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dental Hygiene Cleanings Houston
Professional dental cleanings and hygiene care from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Comprehensive exams, periodontal charting, X-rays, and oral cancer screening.

item.title: Dental Sealants
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Sealants in Houston, TX

Dental sealants are thin, protective plastic coatings applied to the chewing surfaces of the back teeth (molars and premolars) to prevent cavities. The deep pits and grooves of molars are difficult to clean thoroughly and are prime sites for cavity development. Sealants fill in these grooves, creating a smooth surface that sheds food and bacteria easily.

The application process is quick and painless — no drilling required. The tooth surface is cleaned, conditioned, and dried, and the liquid sealant material is flowed into the grooves and hardened with a curing light. Sealants can last 5–10 years with proper care and can be reapplied when worn.

Who Should Get Sealants?

  • Children aged 6–14 when permanent molars and premolars erupt
  • Adults with deep grooves and no existing decay or fillings in back teeth
  • Patients at higher risk for cavities

Sealants are one of the most cost-effective preventive treatments available. Call (713) 464-1887 to ask about sealants for your child or yourself.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Dental Sealants Houston TX
Protect back teeth from cavities with dental sealants from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Quick, painless, and long-lasting cavity prevention for kids and adults.

item.title: Oral Cancer Screenings
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Oral Cancer Screenings in Houston, TX

Oral cancer kills one person every hour in the United States — but when detected early, the five-year survival rate exceeds 80%. Dr. Cimo performs an oral cancer screening at every comprehensive and recall exam using advanced UV light technology to identify tissue changes that may not be visible to the naked eye. The screening is painless and takes only a few minutes.

Risk factors for oral cancer include tobacco use (smoking or smokeless), heavy alcohol consumption, HPV infection, excessive sun exposure to the lips, and a history of prior oral cancer. However, 25% of oral cancers occur in patients with no known risk factors — making routine screenings important for everyone.

The Oral Cancer Screening Process

  • Visual and tactile examination of all oral tissues
  • UV light technology to detect abnormal cell changes
  • Evaluation of lymph nodes in the neck
  • Biopsy referral if suspicious lesions are identified

Annual oral cancer screenings save lives. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your exam — or ask Dr. Cimo to include a screening at your next cleaning appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Oral Cancer Screening Houston TX
Early detection oral cancer screenings with UV light technology from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Painless, life-saving screening included at every exam.

item.title: Mouth Guards
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Custom Mouth Guards in Houston, TX

Athletic mouth guards protect your teeth, gums, lips, and jaw from impact injuries during contact and collision sports. A custom-fitted mouth guard from Dr. Cimo offers far superior protection, comfort, and fit compared to boil-and-bite store-bought guards. It is made from a precise impression of your teeth, allowing you to breathe and communicate normally while wearing it.

The American Dental Association recommends mouth guards for all contact and collision sports including football, hockey, basketball, baseball, soccer, martial arts, wrestling, and more. Athletes who do not wear mouth guards are 60 times more likely to suffer dental injuries.

Custom vs. Store-Bought Guards

FeatureCustomStore-Bought
FitPreciseGeneric
ProtectionSuperiorModerate
ComfortHighLow
Durability3–5 years1 season

Protect your smile this season. Call (713) 464-1887 to get your custom athletic mouth guard.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Custom Mouth Guards Houston TX
Custom athletic mouth guards for sports protection from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Superior fit, comfort, and protection compared to store-bought guards.

item.title: Night Guards
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Custom Night Guards in Houston, TX

Bruxism — grinding or clenching your teeth during sleep — affects up to 10% of adults and can cause significant damage to the teeth, jaw joints, and muscles over time. Symptoms include worn-down teeth, cracked enamel, jaw soreness, headaches, and disrupted sleep. A custom night guard from Dr. Cimo creates a protective barrier between the upper and lower teeth, absorbing the forces of grinding and preventing damage.

Unlike over-the-counter night guards, a custom guard is fabricated from a precise impression of your teeth. It fits perfectly, is comfortable to wear, and is made from high-quality hard acrylic that can withstand years of use. Most patients adapt quickly and find they sleep better when wearing their night guard.

Signs You May Be Grinding

  • Worn, flattened, or chipped teeth
  • Jaw pain or tightness in the morning
  • Headaches, especially in the temples
  • Sensitive teeth with no decay
  • Your partner hears grinding sounds at night

A custom night guard is a simple investment that can save your teeth from thousands of dollars in damage. Call (713) 464-1887 to get fitted for yours.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Night Guards for Bruxism Houston
Protect your teeth from grinding and clenching with a custom night guard from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Relieve jaw pain and prevent enamel damage.

item.title: Occlusal Adjustment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Occlusal Adjustment in Houston, TX

An occlusal adjustment — also called bite equilibration — is the reshaping or selective removal of small amounts of enamel from the biting surfaces of teeth to create a more balanced, even bite. When your bite is uneven, certain teeth bear more force than they should, leading to wear, fractures, tooth sensitivity, jaw pain, and TMJ problems.

Using articulating paper and digital bite analysis, Dr. Cimo identifies precisely which teeth are hitting prematurely or bearing excess force. He then uses a fine dental bur to adjust the high spots in small, precise increments until the bite forces are evenly distributed. The procedure is typically completed in one appointment with little to no discomfort.

Occlusal Adjustment May Help With

  • Uneven bite after a new crown, filling, or other restoration
  • Loose or sore teeth from bite trauma
  • TMJ pain related to bite imbalance
  • Teeth that are wearing unevenly
  • Preparation for night guard or orthodontic treatment

Call (713) 464-1887 if you have noticed a change in your bite or are experiencing jaw pain.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Occlusal Adjustment Houston TX
Balance your bite with an occlusal adjustment from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Relieve tooth sensitivity, jaw pain, and TMJ discomfort from uneven bite forces.

item.title: TMJ Treatment
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
TMJ Treatment in Houston, TX

The temporomandibular joint (TMJ) connects the lower jaw to the skull and is responsible for all jaw movements — chewing, speaking, and yawning. When this joint or the surrounding muscles become inflamed or damaged, it causes a condition called temporomandibular joint disorder (TMD). TMD is surprisingly common, affecting an estimated 10 million Americans.

Dr. Cimo evaluates and treats TMD with a conservative, non-surgical approach as the first line of therapy. Depending on the cause and severity, treatment may include a custom occlusal splint (night guard), occlusal adjustment, physical therapy referral, anti-inflammatory medication, or Botox injections to relax overactive jaw muscles.

Symptoms of TMJ Disorder

  • Jaw pain or soreness, especially in the morning
  • Clicking, popping, or grating sounds when opening or closing the mouth
  • Locking of the jaw in an open or closed position
  • Headaches, ear pain, or neck aches
  • Difficulty chewing or a sudden change in how teeth fit together

Don't suffer in silence. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a TMJ evaluation with Dr. Cimo.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | TMJ Treatment in Houston TX
Relieve TMJ disorder pain and jaw dysfunction with expert treatment from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Splints, adjustments, and conservative TMD therapy.

item.title: CEREC Same-Day Crowns
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
CEREC Same-Day Crowns in Houston, TX

CEREC (Chairside Economical Restoration of Esthetic Ceramics) technology allows Dr. Cimo to design and mill a porcelain crown, inlay, onlay, or veneer in a single appointment — eliminating the need for temporary restorations and multiple visits. Using a digital intraoral scanner, 3D design software, and an in-office milling machine, your permanent ceramic restoration is ready in about 90 minutes.

CEREC restorations are made from a solid block of high-strength dental ceramic that matches the natural color of your teeth. Because the crown is milled from a single block of material, it is stronger and more precise than traditionally fabricated restorations in many cases. Patients love eliminating the inconvenience of temporary crowns and second appointments.

CEREC Benefits

  • Same-day crown — no temporaries, no second visit
  • No messy impressions — digital scanning only
  • Natural-looking, tooth-colored ceramic
  • Precision-milled for an accurate, comfortable fit
  • High-strength material for durability

Call (713) 464-1887 to ask if CEREC is available for your crown or restoration.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | CEREC Same-Day Crowns Houston TX
Get a porcelain crown in a single visit with CEREC technology at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. No temporaries, no second appointment — done in 90 minutes.

item.title: Digital X-Ray
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Digital X-Ray Technology in Houston, TX

Brittmoore Dental uses state-of-the-art digital radiography instead of traditional film X-rays. Digital X-rays emit up to 90% less radiation than conventional film X-rays, produce images instantly on a computer screen, and allow Dr. Cimo to magnify, enhance, and share images with patients for better education and communication.

Digital X-rays allow Dr. Cimo to detect cavities between teeth, bone loss, infections, tumors, and developmental abnormalities that are not visible during a clinical exam. High-resolution digital images are stored electronically in your record, making it easy to compare to previous X-rays to track changes over time.

Types of Digital X-Rays We Use

  • Bitewing X-rays – Detect cavities between teeth; taken at most recall visits
  • Periapical X-rays – Show the entire tooth from crown to root tip
  • Panoramic X-rays (Panorex) – Full-mouth view for implant planning, wisdom teeth, and jaw evaluation
  • 3D Cone Beam CT (i-CAT) – Three-dimensional imaging for implants, surgical planning, and complex cases

Our digital X-ray technology means better care with less radiation. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Digital X-Rays Houston TX
Digital dental X-rays at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Up to 90% less radiation than film, instant images, and better diagnostic accuracy for your care.

item.title: i-CAT 3D Imaging
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
i-CAT 3D Cone Beam Imaging in Houston, TX

The i-CAT is a cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) scanner that produces detailed, three-dimensional images of your teeth, jaw, sinuses, nerve canals, and surrounding structures in a single 8–40 second scan. Unlike traditional 2D X-rays, 3D imaging allows Dr. Cimo to see bone density, root anatomy, and spatial relationships with extraordinary precision — all at a lower radiation dose than a medical CT scan.

3D imaging is used for dental implant planning, wisdom tooth evaluation, airway analysis, orthodontic treatment planning, endodontic diagnosis, and surgical planning. The i-CAT is integrated with the SimPlant planning software to create a virtual surgical guide for precise implant placement.

Applications of i-CAT Imaging

  • Dental implant site evaluation and surgical planning
  • Assessment of impacted wisdom teeth and canines
  • Sinus evaluation for sinus lift procedures
  • Root and nerve canal anatomy for endodontics
  • Temporomandibular joint (TMJ) assessment
  • Airway evaluation for sleep apnea

Call (713) 464-1887 to ask whether 3D imaging is recommended for your treatment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | i-CAT 3D Cone Beam Imaging Houston
Advanced 3D cone beam CT imaging with the i-CAT system at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Precise 3D data for implant planning, wisdom teeth, and complex diagnoses.

item.title: Panorex X-Ray
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Panorex X-Ray in Houston, TX

A Panorex (panoramic radiograph) is a single, wide-view X-ray that captures all of your teeth, upper and lower jaws, sinuses, TMJ joints, and nasal cavity in one image. The panoramic machine rotates around your head while you remain stationary, taking about 20 seconds to complete. It provides a "big picture" view of your entire oral and maxillofacial anatomy.

Panorex X-rays are particularly useful for evaluating wisdom teeth, planning dental implants, assessing developing teeth in children, identifying jaw cysts or tumors, evaluating the TMJ joints, and checking the overall health of the jawbones. Dr. Cimo recommends a panoramic X-ray every 3–5 years as part of your comprehensive dental records.

What a Panorex Can Reveal

  • Impacted or developing wisdom teeth
  • Bone level and density throughout both jaws
  • Jaw joint abnormalities
  • Sinus conditions
  • Cysts, tumors, or other pathology
  • Nerve canal location for implant planning

Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your comprehensive dental exam including panoramic X-ray.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Panorex Panoramic X-Ray Houston
Full-mouth panoramic X-ray (Panorex) at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. A single wide-view image of all teeth, jaws, sinuses, and TMJ for comprehensive diagnosis.

item.title: Intraoral Camera
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Intraoral Camera in Houston, TX

An intraoral camera is a small, pen-sized device with a built-in camera and light source that allows Dr. Cimo to capture high-definition video and still images inside your mouth. The images are instantly displayed on a chairside monitor, giving you a clear, magnified view of your own teeth and gums — the same view Dr. Cimo is seeing.

Intraoral cameras improve patient education, allow Dr. Cimo to document clinical findings accurately, and help patients understand the nature of their dental problems. When patients can see a cracked tooth or a failing filling with their own eyes, they are more engaged in their own care and confident in their treatment decisions.

Uses of the Intraoral Camera

  • Showing patients the condition of their teeth and gums
  • Documenting and monitoring cavities, cracks, and wear
  • Recording before-and-after treatment results
  • Identifying areas of gum inflammation or recession
  • Insurance documentation with photographic evidence

At Brittmoore Dental, we believe an informed patient is a healthier patient. Call (713) 464-1887 to experience our patient-centered technology.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Intraoral Camera Houston TX
See inside your own mouth with our intraoral camera at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. High-definition images help you understand your diagnosis and treatment plan.

item.title: Dental Laser Technology
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Laser Technology in Houston, TX

Dental lasers allow Dr. Cimo to perform many soft tissue procedures with greater precision, less bleeding, and faster healing than traditional scalpels. The laser energy sterilizes the treatment site simultaneously, reducing the risk of post-operative infection. Many laser procedures require little or no anesthesia and can be completed with minimal discomfort.

Dr. Cimo uses a soft-tissue diode laser for procedures including gum contouring, frenectomies, crown lengthening, treatment of aphthous ulcers, biopsies, and bacterial reduction in periodontal pockets during scaling and root planing. Laser treatment is a comfortable, minimally invasive option that many patients prefer.

Dental Laser Applications

  • Gum contouring and reshaping
  • Frenectomy (lip and tongue tie release)
  • Crown lengthening
  • Soft tissue biopsy
  • Aphthous ulcer (canker sore) treatment
  • Decontamination of periodontal pockets
  • Uncovering implants

item.title: WaterLase Dental Laser
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
WaterLase Dental Laser in Houston, TX

The WaterLase is an advanced all-tissue dental laser that combines laser energy with a water spray to cut and reshape both hard tissue (tooth enamel and bone) and soft tissue (gum and mucosa). Unlike traditional dental drills, the WaterLase creates no heat buildup, no vibration, and far less noise — making it a more comfortable experience for many patients, often with reduced or no anesthesia needed for cavity preparations.

The water spray simultaneously cools the tissue and clears the treatment area, minimizing collateral damage and dramatically reducing post-operative discomfort. The WaterLase is used for cavity preparation, crown preparation, gum procedures, bone surgery, apicoectomies, and more.

WaterLase Benefits

  • No drill vibration or heat generation
  • Many procedures require little or no anesthesia
  • Treats both hard and soft tissue
  • Reduced post-operative pain and swelling
  • Faster healing due to precise, sterile cuts

Call (713) 464-1887 to ask whether WaterLase is right for your upcoming procedure.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | WaterLase Laser Dentistry Houston
Experience drill-free dentistry with WaterLase at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. All-tissue laser for cavities, gum procedures, and more — often no anesthesia needed.

item.title: SimPlant Implant Planning
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
SimPlant 3D Implant Planning in Houston, TX

SimPlant is an advanced 3D implant planning software that Dr. Cimo uses in conjunction with the i-CAT cone beam CT scanner to plan dental implant placement with precision and predictability. SimPlant allows Dr. Cimo to virtually place implants on a 3D model of the patient's jaw, evaluating bone quality, bone quantity, nerve canal proximity, sinus location, and ideal implant angle and depth before a single incision is made.

After the virtual plan is finalized, a surgical guide (stent) can be fabricated to translate the computer-planned positions directly into the patient's mouth at the time of surgery. This guided implant placement approach maximizes safety, minimizes complications, and improves long-term implant success rates.

Benefits of SimPlant Planning

  • Pre-surgical identification of optimal implant position
  • Avoidance of nerves, sinuses, and adjacent roots
  • Surgical guide for precise, predictable placement
  • Reduced surgical time and patient discomfort
  • Better outcomes and fewer complications

Call (713) 464-1887 to learn how SimPlant-guided implant surgery ensures the best possible outcome for your implant treatment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | SimPlant 3D Implant Planning Houston
Precise 3D implant planning with SimPlant software at Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Virtual surgical planning for safer, more predictable dental implant placement.

item.title: Osstell Implant Stability
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Osstell Implant Stability Measurement in Houston, TX

The Osstell device uses Resonance Frequency Analysis (RFA) to objectively measure implant stability at placement and during the healing phase. A small SmartPeg is attached to the implant, and the Osstell device measures the resonance frequency to produce an Implant Stability Quotient (ISQ) score from 1–100. Higher scores indicate greater stability and bone-to-implant contact.

By tracking ISQ scores over time, Dr. Cimo can make data-driven decisions about when an implant is ready for loading (crown placement), minimizing the risk of implant failure and ensuring that osseointegration is complete before restoring the implant. This objective measurement replaces guesswork with science.

Benefits of Osstell Monitoring

  • Objective measurement of osseointegration progress
  • Earlier loading when stability criteria are met
  • Identification of implants at risk before they fail
  • Better documentation and patient communication

At Brittmoore Dental, we use the best technology to ensure the best implant outcomes. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your implant consultation.

item.title: Electric Handpieces
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Electric Handpieces at Brittmoore Dental

Electric dental handpieces (drills) offer significant advantages over traditional air-driven handpieces. They operate at a consistent, controlled speed without the variation caused by changes in air pressure, providing more precise, smoother cutting — especially important for crown preparations and cavity work requiring close tolerances.

Electric handpieces are also significantly quieter than air-driven models, contributing to a more relaxed dental experience. The torque is constant and controllable, which means less vibration, better tactile feedback for Dr. Cimo, and smoother, more uniform preparations that improve the fit and longevity of your restorations.

Benefits of Electric Handpieces

  • More consistent, controlled speed for precise cutting
  • Less vibration and noise than air-driven drills
  • Better tactile feedback for the dentist
  • Smoother tooth preparations for better-fitting restorations
  • More comfortable for patients

Brittmoore Dental invests in the best equipment to provide the highest quality restorations and the most comfortable experience possible. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your appointment.

item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Lluma Ultra Cone Beam CT Scanner in Houston, TX

The Lluma Ultra is a next-generation cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) scanner that produces ultra-high-resolution 3D images of the dentition, jawbones, sinuses, and airway. With its wide field of view and low radiation dose, it provides exceptional diagnostic information for complex dental and surgical planning cases.

The Lluma Ultra complements our i-CAT system and provides Dr. Cimo with the 3D anatomical data needed for precise implant planning, endodontic diagnosis, orthognathic surgical planning, and airway assessment. The scan is completed in seconds, and images are available immediately for review with the patient.

Clinical Applications

  • Full-arch implant treatment planning
  • Complex surgical planning (bone grafts, sinus lifts)
  • Evaluation of complex root canal anatomy
  • Airway and sleep apnea assessment
  • Assessment of impacted teeth and jaw pathology

Brittmoore Dental is equipped with the most advanced imaging technology in Houston. Call (713) 464-1887 to schedule your consultation.

item.title: Post-Op Instructions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Post-Operative Instructions

Please follow these instructions carefully after your dental procedure to ensure proper healing and minimize complications. If you have any questions or concerns, call our office at (713) 464-1887.

After Tooth Extractions

  • Bite firmly on gauze for 30–45 minutes; replace if soaked with fresh gauze
  • Do NOT rinse, spit, or use straws for 24 hours (disrupts clot formation)
  • Apply ice packs for 20 minutes on / 20 minutes off for the first 24 hours
  • Take pain medication as directed before numbness wears off
  • Eat soft foods: yogurt, soup, mashed potatoes, smoothies (no straws)
  • No smoking or tobacco for at least 72 hours

After Root Canal Therapy

  • Some tenderness for 2–5 days is normal; over-the-counter pain relievers help
  • Avoid chewing on the treated side until a permanent crown is placed
  • Call if swelling, severe pain, or a visible bump develops near the tooth

After Crown or Veneer Placement

  • Avoid sticky or hard foods for 24 hours after cementation
  • Some sensitivity to hot, cold, and pressure is normal for 1–2 weeks
  • If your bite feels uneven, call to schedule an adjustment

After Scaling & Root Planing

  • Avoid hard, crunchy, or spicy foods for 24 hours
  • Use a prescription mouth rinse if provided
  • Gums will be sore for 1–3 days — gentle brushing is still important

Questions? Call (713) 464-1887. For after-hours emergencies, call the emergency line provided at your appointment.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | Post-Op Instructions Houston TX
Post-operative care instructions for extractions, root canals, crowns, and more from Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Follow these guidelines for proper healing.

item.title: After Dental Implant Surgery
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After Dental Implant Surgery

Please follow these post-operative instructions carefully after your dental implant placement procedure. Proper home care is essential for successful osseointegration and implant longevity. Call (713) 464-1887 with any concerns.

First 24 Hours

  • Bite gently on gauze for 30–60 minutes; apply fresh gauze if bleeding continues
  • Do NOT rinse, spit forcefully, or use straws for 24 hours
  • Apply ice to the outside of the face: 20 minutes on / 20 minutes off
  • Take prescribed antibiotics and pain medication as directed
  • Eat only soft foods: yogurt, mashed potatoes, smoothies, soup
  • Rest and avoid strenuous physical activity

Days 2–7

  • Rinse gently with warm salt water (1/2 tsp salt in 8 oz water) 2–3 times daily starting 24 hours after surgery
  • Brush teeth normally but avoid the implant site
  • Swelling may peak at 48–72 hours then gradually resolve
  • Continue soft diet for 1–2 weeks
  • No smoking — tobacco use significantly increases implant failure risk

Long-Term Care

  • Avoid biting hard objects (ice, hard candy) on the implant
  • Maintain excellent oral hygiene around the implant
  • Return for all scheduled follow-up appointments

Call immediately if you experience severe pain, fever, excessive bleeding, or pus around the implant site.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | After Dental Implant Surgery Instructions
Post-operative care after dental implant surgery from Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Follow these instructions for proper healing and implant success.

item.title: After Impacted Tooth Removal
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After Impacted Tooth Removal

Recovery from the removal of an impacted tooth is generally similar to recovery from a regular extraction but may take a few extra days due to the surgical nature of the procedure. Please follow these instructions carefully. Call (713) 464-1887 if you have questions or concerns.

Bleeding

Some oozing is normal for up to 24 hours. Bite firmly on gauze for 45 minutes. If bleeding persists, fold fresh gauze into a firm pad and bite down for another 30 minutes. Avoid rinsing or spitting for 24 hours to protect the clot.

Swelling & Bruising

Swelling typically peaks at 48–72 hours and gradually resolves over 5–7 days. Apply ice for the first 24 hours, then switch to moist heat. Bruising of the cheek or neck is normal and will fade within 1–2 weeks.

Pain Management

Take prescribed pain medication as directed, beginning before the numbness wears off. Over-the-counter ibuprofen (if not contraindicated) can be alternated with acetaminophen for added relief.

Diet

  • Liquid and soft diet for the first 24–48 hours
  • No straws, carbonated beverages, or alcohol for 72 hours
  • Advance to normal diet as comfort allows, usually by day 5–7

Oral Hygiene

  • Do not brush the surgical site for 24 hours
  • Gently rinse with warm salt water starting 24 hours after surgery, 4–5 times daily

If severe pain, fever, or swelling worsens after the third day, call immediately — this may indicate dry socket or infection.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | After Impacted Tooth Removal Instructions
Post-op care instructions after impacted tooth removal from Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Manage bleeding, swelling, and pain for a smooth recovery.

item.title: After Wisdom Tooth Removal
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
After Wisdom Tooth Removal

Wisdom tooth extraction recovery typically takes 3–7 days for most patients. Following these instructions carefully will help minimize your discomfort, prevent dry socket, and promote healing. Call (713) 464-1887 if you have concerns.

Day of Surgery

  • Bite on gauze for 45–60 minutes after leaving the office; replace if necessary
  • Go home, rest, and avoid physical activity for the remainder of the day
  • Apply ice packs to the outside of your face: 20 minutes on / 20 minutes off for 6–8 hours
  • Take all prescribed medications as directed
  • Eat soft foods only: applesauce, yogurt, mashed potatoes, soup broth
  • Do NOT use straws, smoke, or rinse forcefully for 72 hours

Days 2–5

  • Rinse gently with warm salt water (1/2 tsp in 8 oz) 3–4 times daily, especially after meals
  • Swelling and stiffness in the jaw are normal and peak around day 2–3
  • Continue a soft diet; introduce soft, solid foods when comfortable
  • Keep extraction sites clean but avoid disturbing clots

Warning Signs — Call Immediately

  • Severe, worsening pain on day 3 or 4 (possible dry socket)
  • Fever above 101°F
  • Excessive swelling that is expanding, not resolving
  • Difficulty breathing or swallowing

Most patients return to school or work within 2–3 days. Full healing takes 2–4 weeks.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | After Wisdom Tooth Removal Instructions
Post-op care after wisdom tooth removal from Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Prevent dry socket and manage swelling for a fast 3–7 day recovery.

item.title: Surgical Instructions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Surgical Instructions — Brittmoore Dental

These general surgical instructions apply to all oral surgical procedures performed at our office. Specific procedure instructions will be provided at your appointment. For any concerns, call (713) 464-1887.

Before Your Surgery

  • If IV or oral sedation is planned, do not eat or drink for 8 hours before your appointment
  • Arrange for a responsible adult driver if sedation is planned
  • Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing
  • Fill any prescriptions in advance so they are ready when you return home
  • Inform our office of all medications you take, especially blood thinners

After Your Surgery

  • Do not rinse, spit, or use straws for the first 24 hours
  • Avoid strenuous exercise for 48–72 hours
  • Elevate your head while sleeping for the first 2 nights to reduce swelling
  • Take all prescribed medications as directed
  • Apply ice for the first 24 hours; switch to moist heat on day 2–3 for stiffness
  • Eat only soft foods for the first few days

Healing Expectations

  • Mild to moderate bleeding for the first few hours is normal
  • Swelling typically peaks at 48–72 hours then gradually resolves
  • Bruising can appear on the face and neck — this is normal and resolves in 1–2 weeks

Return to our office for all scheduled follow-up appointments. Early follow-up allows Dr. Cimo to verify healing and address any concerns promptly.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS | Surgical Instructions Houston TX
Pre and post-operative surgical instructions from Brittmoore Dental in Houston, TX. Follow these guidelines before and after oral surgery for the best outcomes.

item.title: Meet Dr. Cimo
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Meet Dr. Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD

Dr. Philip J. Cimo is a native Houstonian who has been serving the West Houston community since 2004. A graduate of the University of Texas Dental Branch (now UTHealth Houston School of Dentistry), he earned his Doctor of Dental Surgery degree and has continued to invest in advanced education ever since. He holds a Fellowship in the Academy of General Dentistry (FAGD) — a distinction earned by fewer than 6% of general dentists in the United States, reflecting hundreds of hours of continuing education and a rigorous examination.

Dr. Cimo served in the United States Army as a dental officer, providing care to active-duty personnel and their families. He is certified in IV sedation and trained in advanced implant surgery, periodontics, and endodontics — allowing him to provide a full spectrum of care under one roof. He is a member of the American Dental Association, the Texas Dental Association, and the Houston District Dental Society.

Outside the office, Dr. Cimo enjoys spending time with his family, fishing, hunting, and staying involved in the Houston community. His warm, genuine approach and commitment to honesty put even the most anxious patients at ease. Call (713) 464-1887 to meet Dr. Cimo and experience the Brittmoore Dental difference.

item.title: Q&A
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Common Questions & Answers — Brittmoore Dental

Below are answers to some of the most frequently asked questions about our practice and dental care. Don't see your question answered here? Call (713) 464-1887 — we are happy to help.

How often should I visit the dentist?

Most patients benefit from a cleaning and exam every six months. Patients with active gum disease or a history of frequent cavities may need to be seen every 3–4 months.

Do you accept my insurance?

We accept most major dental insurance plans as out-of-network providers and will file your claim on your behalf. We also accept CareCredit and Sunbit financing. Call our office to verify your specific benefits.

What do I do if I have a dental emergency?

Call our office at (713) 464-1887 immediately. We reserve time in our schedule for dental emergencies and will make every effort to see you the same day.

Are dental X-rays safe?

Yes. Digital X-rays used at our office emit up to 90% less radiation than traditional film X-rays. The diagnostic benefits far outweigh the minimal radiation exposure.

How long do dental implants last?

With proper care and maintenance, dental implants can last a lifetime. The crown placed on the implant typically lasts 15–25 years before needing replacement.

Do you see children?

Yes! We welcome patients of all ages. We recommend a child's first dental visit by age one, or within 6 months of the first tooth's eruption.

item.title: Dental Implant FAQ
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Dental Implant FAQ

Find answers to the most common questions about dental implants. For more in-depth information, visit our Dental Implant FAQs page or call (713) 464-1887 to schedule a consultation with Dr. Cimo.

What is a dental implant?

A dental implant is a titanium post surgically placed into the jawbone to replace the root of a missing tooth. Once it integrates with the bone (osseointegration), a porcelain crown is attached to create a permanent, natural-looking tooth replacement.

Am I a candidate for dental implants?

Most adults in good general health are candidates for implants. Adequate bone volume, healthy gums, and no uncontrolled systemic diseases are the key requirements. Bone grafting can often create the necessary bone if it is deficient.

How long do implants last?

With proper care, dental implants can last a lifetime. The implant post is permanent; the crown may need replacement after 15–25 years.

Does the implant procedure hurt?

No. The procedure is performed under local anesthesia (with sedation available), and most patients report that placement is far more comfortable than they expected. Post-operative discomfort is typically managed with over-the-counter pain relievers.

How much do implants cost?

A single implant typically ranges from $3,000–$5,000 for the complete restoration (post + abutment + crown). Financing is available through CareCredit and Sunbit. Call for a specific quote.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-implant-faqs.php

item.title: Endo Microscope
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Endodontic Microscope in Houston, TX

The surgical endodontic microscope provides 4–30x magnification and coaxial fiber-optic illumination, allowing Dr. Cimo to see root canal anatomy, calcified canals, fractured files, and perforation sites with extraordinary detail that is simply not visible to the naked eye. Using the microscope during root canal treatment and endodontic microsurgery significantly improves outcomes and reduces the rate of complications.

With the endodontic microscope, Dr. Cimo can identify additional canals that might be missed, locate the precise extent of a fracture, perform ultra-precise apicoectomy surgery, and remove separated instruments from canals — procedures that without the scope carry a much higher risk of failure.

How the Microscope Benefits You

  • Greater accuracy in locating and treating all root canals
  • Better outcomes in complex endodontic cases
  • Minimally invasive surgical technique during apicoectomy
  • Ability to identify and treat issues other dentists may miss

Dr. Cimo is committed to using every available tool to save your natural teeth. Call (713) 464-1887 for complex endodontic care.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: template-service.php
Philip J. Cimo DDS, FAGD | Endodontic Microscope Houston TX
Precision root canal treatment with an endodontic microscope from Dr. Philip Cimo in Houston, TX. Magnified visualization for better outcomes in complex endodontic cases.
